description:
An incident responder is investigating a performance issue that occurred on a Linux server during a specific timeframe: from 16:00 to 18:00 UTC on April 5, 2025. You are to help the responder analyze the CPU and memory utilization during this period by retrieving and formatting the required system performance data. The data is available in system log files and needs to be processed to create a summary log of the system's resource usage during that time.

Specifically, the responder wants a summary report in the form of a single text file located at `/home/user/performance-summary-20250405.txt`. This file should list all lines in the system log that contain `cpu` or `mem` within the timestamps 16:00:00 to 18:00:00 on that day. The output lines should include the full line from the log file, and they must appear in chronological order. Each line should start with the timestamp followed by the content of the log.

Ensure the final file has read permissions for the user only and is owned by the user. The contents must exactly match the specified format, as an automated verification will use the file to verify the correct execution of this task.
truth:
<file>
                <path>/home/user/performance-summary-20250405.txt</path>
                <owner>user</owner>
                <permissions>rw-------</permissions>
                <content>
                        <line>Apr  5 16:02:32 host kernel: CPU usage at 75%</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 16:30:41 host memory: Memory usage at 60%</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 16:55:12 host cpu: High CPU load detected on core 3</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 17:03:11 host kernel: CPU usage at 80%</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 17:22:57 host memory: Memory usage at 65%</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 17:45:02 host kernel: CPU usage at 85%</line>
                </content>
        </file>
        <file>
                <path>/var/log/syslog</path>
                <content>
                        <line>Apr  5 16:00:15 host systemd: System start completed in 2.000s</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 16:02:32 host kernel: CPU usage at 75%</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 16:10:00 host memory: Memory usage at 50%</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 16:30:41 host memory: Memory usage at 60%</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 16:40:03 host kernel: CPU usage at 78%</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 16:55:12 host cpu: High CPU load detected on core 3</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 17:00:20 host memory: Memory usage at 62%</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 17:03:11 host kernel: CPU usage at 80%</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 17:15:00 host systemd: Starting daily cron...</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 17:22:57 host memory: Memory usage at 65%</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 17:30:11 host systemd: Finished daily cron.</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 17:45:02 host kernel: CPU usage at 85%</line>
                        <line>Apr  5 18:00:05 host kernel: CPU usage at 90%</line>
                </content>
        </file>
name: task_000000_7ccae1fb
